dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding to a MVC in the city of Richmond.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Just to clarify for the record, we are dispatched for a motor vehicle collision.
My left wrist hurts bad. I hit it when my car crashed.
Ma'am, can you tell me your name and where we are right now?
Maria... and we are in Richmond, Virginia.
Keep your eyes open, please. No need to close them.
Tell me how many fingers I'm holding up.
You're holding up two.
You had your airbag deploy, right? We're going to check your airway.
Respirations are 16 per minute. Breathing remains stable with no signs of distress.
Pulse is 97 beats per minute. Skin is warm and dry with no cyanosis noted.
You are awake, alert, and oriented to person, place, time, and situation.
Maria, can we ask about your regular medical conditions?
I take Xanax, Lipitor, Abilify, and Ambien regularly.
I have a severe headache and a very painful left wrist.
It'd help if you could tell us your age and approximate weight.
How would you rate the pain in your left wrist on a scale of 0 to 10?
It's a 10. It gets worse when I move it.
Your forehead has an abrasion with a little swelling.
The skin is normal, warm, and dry with no rashes or lesions noted.
I need to check your abdomen-that okay with you?
It's tender in the center, maybe a 6 on the pain scale.
Your left wrist is swollen with a laceration showing.
Any back pain or tenderness?
No, I feel fine in my back.
You were an unrestrained driver in a head-on collision. There's possible contact with a utility pole. You had major damage to the driver's side.
Pulse remains at 97.
Respirations are consistently at 16.
Blood pressure is 104/67.
SpO2 is 97% on room air.
We've applied the EKG now. There is no evidence of ischemia or arrhythmia at this time.
We're placing a cervical collar for protection-there's currently no neck pain, but we want to be cautious.
Bleeding is controlled on your wrist with a pressure dressing-positive laceration located and managed.
Let's splint your wrist for support. Try not to move it too much.
I will stay still.
We are following the general trauma protocol for transport.
Pulse is now 93, still stable and within normal range.
Blood pressure has increased to 111/81-nothing alarming noted there.
SpO2 is 98 now with an EKG monitor in place and functioning well.
Did you lose consciousness at any point after the crash?
No, I stayed fully aware the entire time.
We'll be taking you to VCU Health Systems.
I'll assist you standing on the stretcher now-okay?
Yes, I'll keep still.
They're ready in the trauma room.
This is Maria, 40 years old, with wrist pain and a head abrasion.
We applied a cervical collar and splinted the left wrist-transport was smooth.
No back pain or tenderness reported at any point during assessment or transport.
She remained alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation during the entire process.
A history of major depression and mild asthma has been noted in her medical records predating the event.
Cervical collar applied and secured for spinal immobilization with no resistance noted from patient during placement.
Left wrist has reduced range of motion due to pain, as reported by the patient herself during exam and transport phases.
You were moved to the unit using an x-port method-no incident occurred during the transfer, and patient was observed to remain stable during movement.
Let me double-check that your wrist is still stable and the splint remains secure to prevent any displacement of the injury during transit.
It's still in place and feels secure-there's no movement or instability reported from this item at this time.
Pressures are still holding on the dressing-no fresh bleeding observed, and dressing remains intact without any changes noted for the last 45 minutes post-application.
Any numbness or tingling in the left arm or hand at this moment, Maria?
No, though moving it still screams in pain-no reported numbness or tingling experienced at this time in the distal extremities of this limb.
No other issues reported at this point aside from reported pain in locations already discussed during initial and all intermediate assessments prior to reassessment.

Pulse is steady and within normal range with no concerning deviation from documented initial presentation at any time observed to date-documented beat by beat as per monitoring equipment readings during transport phase.

Breathing remains regular with no signs of distress, consistent with ongoing monitoring by the partner until now-no difficulty in speaking observed during reassessment activity or in responses from patient until this utterance timeline.
Any chest pain or nausea or vomiting experienced at any prior documentation stage or as of now, per your knowledge, Maria?
No-just my head and my wrist are the issues as mentioned. No involvement of other organ systems or pain beyond this location indicates otherwise for me personally at this moment in time.

And no difficulty with your pelvis or abdomen, correct? No signs of distension or tenderness in those areas beyond already documented information from previous stages of interaction and questioning to date up to here now in conversation log enslavement or interpretation of EPCR events.

The abdomen feels a bit sore in the center-regarding this organ system, that's the maximum extent of any odd or painful sensation from current report across existing sample of assessment that exist mutually for this case at such given point during inquiry.
Any eye pain or vision issues previously to this account or during hours of documentation at this time?

No, just an abrasion on my forehead-per detailed account during inspection stage gave further confirmation of harmless nature despite pain involved in sustained proximity from crash mechanics as described by self.

SpO2 is 98, still good for room air-patient continued normal saturation as assessed from all continuing phases until current steady_state indication of this numerical value remains confirmed until second documentation input.

Blood pressure remains steady, which is good with regard to signs of hemodynamic compromise or sudden drop causing concern that would trigger rerouting or re-prioritization of actions undertaken shall not happen here]
Is the pain in your wrist still at 10, as you previously rated during initial stages of this encounter and transportation process, Maria?

Yes, it's still very painful-however likely not more than initial assessment but remains intensely felt upon slight pressure examination externally from hands or bandages with no suggestion otherwise from prior before now.
No noted hand deformities, fractures, or signs of circulation deficits-hand remains pink with palpable pulses for now based on assessment...
Just bandaged and splinted-nothing more-not complaining within this range unless specific questioning occurs after finding new developments or maybe not at all..
No signs of neck instability or deformity-this area is functioning and structurally strong according to observations to current date.
Airway patency remains intact with no stridor, distortion of sound or visual obstruction detected so far-which is positive note to consider here currently...
Glucose testing was not possible due to environmental or equipment limitations-but no symptoms of hypoglycemia reported at any time until this utterance is made.
All necessary checks have been completed-transport can begin with team satisfaction on all systems and parameters safe to proceed.
We're taking you to VCU now for more specialized care-confirmed with patient during placement into unit with destination decision dialog performed before this statement creation.

Preparing the stretcher for movement now-teams are in line with finalized instructions regarding transportation methods, team coordination on scene circulation during re-assessment steps added for final verification phase]]
You're secured to the unit now-no issues noted here and past history backing up full compliance during raster-based review times]]
Ready for transport? Double-checking all connections-will do once more to ensure no oversight has taken part in attempting completion of process as intended subdivision]
Ready. I'm just a little worried about my wrist-otherwise accepting of situation and okay with plans laid out in associated response trains of action]]
It's looked after. You're in good hands-this device is working and has passed before final securing indicated]
Transport is safe and secure-no risk of movement during en route phases of extensive full-throttle evaluations pairs]]
ER is on standby for your arrival-teams at facility have been apprised ahead of time as per ground truth event logs]]
Let me do one final round of vital checks-continuing to keep documentation smooth for all subsequent modifications done here]]
Pulse remains at 93, SpO2 at 98-confirmed operational proper reading as per all prior estimates]]
Blood pressure is still under control at 111/81-no lateral talks of unstable readings observed]]
All interventions remain in place and functioning as expected-splints, collars, and existing trauma response measures）
Seems to still be fine-pauses here and then affirmatively says yes to this question）
We're moving the stretcher into the ER section now-with little to no effort both from transporting and receiving sides）
She was seen by the ER staff as confirmed on transfer-handover complete with prior preparation protocols）
There's a starburst pattern on the windshield from the impact-likely caused by the windshield and passenger's head contact）
Maria was assisted standing to the stretcher with support-as noted in narrative）
Confirmed-no loss of consciousness at any point-documented in ePCR）
All systems green. Ready for handoff-no exception in this case found during close loop"/>
